% Dual-majority data macros: C++ majority vs.\ c-nohavoc_std majority (980 shared tasks).
% Needs \usepackage[table]{xcolor} and \usepackage{pgfplotstable}.
% Regenerated from analysis.ipynb -- do not edit. \input once, then cite \dualCNohavocStd... inline.
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTrueTrue}{208}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTrueFalse}{46}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTrueTie}{1}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTrueUnknown}{292}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTrueTotal}{547}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdFalseTrue}{8}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdFalseFalse}{266}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdFalseTie}{2}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdFalseUnknown}{117}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdFalseTotal}{393}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTieTrue}{9}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTieFalse}{2}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTieTie}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTieUnknown}{16}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTieTotal}{27}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdUnknownTrue}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdUnknownFalse}{8}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdUnknownTie}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdUnknownUnknown}{5}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdUnknownTotal}{13}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTotalTrue}{225}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTotalFalse}{322}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTotalTie}{3}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTotalUnknown}{430}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdTotal}{980}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdAgree}{479}
\newcommand{\dualCNohavocStdDisagree}{501}

\ifdefined\dualcolorcells\else\let\dualcolorcells\relax
  % Square data cells: each cell is an explicit \colorbox of side \dualcellw -- a
  % fixed-width \makebox with a strut of the same height, no \fboxsep and no column
  % padding (@{}c@{}), so the shaded boxes are equal squares and tile contiguously.
  % \dualcellw auto-sizes to the widest header so nothing wraps.
  \newlength{\dualcellw}\settowidth{\dualcellw}{unknown}\addtolength{\dualcellw}{0.9em}%
  \newcommand{\dualbox}[2]{{\setlength{\fboxsep}{0pt}%
    \colorbox{black!#1}{\makebox[\dualcellw][c]{%
      \rule[-0.5\dualcellw]{0pt}{\dualcellw}\ifnum#1>50 \color{white}\fi #2}}}}%
  \pgfplotstableset{
    color cells/.style={
      col sep=comma, string type, column type={@{}c@{}},
      % Shade each count on the black! scale, normalised to the per-table max \heatVmax
      % (so the printed value is the raw count but the colour is 0..100). The shade is
      % computed here, at parse time, and baked into \dualbox as a literal.
      postproc cell content/.code={%
        \pgfmathtruncatemacro\dualshade{round(100*(##1/\heatVmax))}%
        \pgfkeysalso{@cell content/.expanded={\noexpand\dualbox{\dualshade}{\noexpand##1}}}%
      },
      columns/x/.style={column name={}, column type={r@{\hskip 0.6em}}, postproc cell content/.code={}},
    },
  }
\fi

\begin{table}[t]
  \centering
  \caption{Dual majority over the 980 tasks shared by the original C++ suite and \texttt{c-nohavoc_std}: rows are the C++ majority verdict, columns the \texttt{c-nohavoc_std} majority verdict; cell shading is the count relative to the table maximum.}
  \label{tab:dual-majority-c-nohavoc_std-macros}
  \def\heatVmax{292}%
  \setlength{\extrarowheight}{0pt}\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{0}% rows tile: each \dualbox sets its own square height
  \pgfplotstabletypeset[color cells]{
x,true,false,tie,unknown
true,\dualCNohavocStdTrueTrue,\dualCNohavocStdTrueFalse,\dualCNohavocStdTrueTie,\dualCNohavocStdTrueUnknown
false,\dualCNohavocStdFalseTrue,\dualCNohavocStdFalseFalse,\dualCNohavocStdFalseTie,\dualCNohavocStdFalseUnknown
tie,\dualCNohavocStdTieTrue,\dualCNohavocStdTieFalse,\dualCNohavocStdTieTie,\dualCNohavocStdTieUnknown
unknown,\dualCNohavocStdUnknownTrue,\dualCNohavocStdUnknownFalse,\dualCNohavocStdUnknownTie,\dualCNohavocStdUnknownUnknown
  }
\end{table}
